data.text/markdown:
Day 1 - Agentic Systems Fundamentals, Business Value, and FDE Judgment

Primary learner entrypoint: modules/day_01_trust_boundary/README.md. Read the customer context and file manifest there before you start the incident. Day 1 is no longer a conceptual tour. You are responding to a real intake failure, proving where the break lives, prototyping the repair in the notebook, and then shipping the fix in the production codebase.

data.text/html: <marimo-callout-output data-html='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><h2 id=\"how-youll-be-scored-today\">How You'll Be Scored Today</h2>\n<table>\n<thead>\n<tr>\n<th>Dimension</th>\n<th style=\"text-align: right;\">Points</th>\n</tr>\n</thead>\n<tbody>\n<tr>\n<td><code>agent_fit_signals</code></td>\n<td style=\"text-align: right;\">25</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td><code>rejection_criteria</code></td>\n<td style=\"text-align: right;\">20</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td><code>waf_trust_tradeoff</code></td>\n<td style=\"text-align: right;\">20</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td><code>business_framing</code></td>\n<td style=\"text-align: right;\">15</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td><code>oral_defense</code></td>\n<td style=\"text-align: right;\">20</td>\n</tr>\n</tbody>\n</table>\n<span class=\"paragraph\">See <a href=\"/workspaces/agentic-accounts-payable-orchestrator/docs/curriculum/ASSESSMENT_RUBRIC.md\">ASSESSMENT_RUBRIC.md</a> for band descriptors and\n<a href=\"/workspaces/agentic-accounts-payable-orchestrator/docs/curriculum/CURRICULUM_MANIFEST.yaml\">CURRICULUM_MANIFEST.yaml</a> for the authoritative day contract.</span></span>"' data-kind='"info"'></marimo-callout-output>

data.text/markdown:
Incident

A valid European supplier invoice is being rejected at the trust boundary after a recent change. The finance team does not care about the theory. They care that a real invoice with mixed separators like 1.250,00 EUR no longer crosses the boundary safely. What success looks like
  • you can prove the failure is deterministic Python, not the model prompt
  • the repaired path still rejects malformed amounts and accepts valid locale input
  • the production tests are green before AP retries the invoice

Portal Investigation

Start in Azure before you blame the parser.
  1. Open the Foundry or Azure OpenAI deployment and confirm the extractor still returns the expected Day 1 candidate keys.
  2. Compare one raw model payload with the failing finance case. Capture the exact amount string the model produced.
  3. Confirm IAM and endpoint health are unchanged so you do not misclassify an identity or networking problem as a parsing bug.
  4. Write down the exact amount literal that crosses from extraction into deterministic normalization.
Infrastructure-first debugging rule
  • Check identity and endpoint health before changing prompts.
  • Check data shape before changing policy.
  • Blame the model only after the deterministic boundary is proven correct.

Lab Repair

Use this section as a scratchpad only. The goal is to prove a safe fix reactively before you touch the production files.

<marimo-callout-output data-html='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\">The prototype failed with:</span>\n<div class=\"language-text codehilite\"><pre><span></span><code>ambiguous mixed-separator amount, reject at boundary: &#39;1.250,00 EUR&#39;\n</code></pre></div></span>"' data-kind='"danger"'></marimo-callout-output>

<marimo-callout-output data-html='"<span class=\"markdown prose dark:prose-invert contents\"><span class=\"paragraph\">The fixture is currently rejected:</span>\n<div class=\"language-text codehilite\"><pre><span></span><code>invoice rejected at intake boundary: ambiguous mixed-separator amount, reject at boundary: &#39;1.000,00 EUR&#39;\n</code></pre></div></span>"' data-kind='"danger"'></marimo-callout-output>

data.text/markdown:
Why This Fails In Prod

List three specific ways this notebook logic fails in an Azure Container App. You must reference at least one Azure limit (memory, timeout, or ephemeral storage) and one concurrency issue.

Codification Bridge

Treat the portal and notebook as evidence, not as the fix.
  • Portal state: the extractor and endpoint health are fine, but the locale-formatted amount crossing the deterministic boundary is not handled safely.
  • Notebook proof: parse_money(...) and the fixture walkthrough prove whether the break lives in normalization or the reject path.
  • Permanent repo change: src/aegisap/day_01/normalizers.py and, if needed, src/aegisap/day_01/service.py.
Rosetta Stone: notebooks/bridges/day01_trust_boundary.md

Production Patch

This section is markdown-only. Do not edit repo files from this notebook. Edit the repo target in your IDE first. Rerun this notebook bootstrap cell after every repo edit so deep_reload_modules(...) reloads the real package imports before you trust the notebook proof again. Write the durable patch in the repo target below, not inside Marimo. Your prototype is only a hypothesis until the real code changes land in the repo. Open the production files in your editor, implement the fix there, and then return here to verify the external code:
  • src/aegisap/day_01/normalizers.py
  • src/aegisap/day_01/service.py if the rejection path or exception handling needs adjustment

Export to Production

  • Which exact amount literal caused the failure?
  • What exact normalization or rejection rule must change in src/aegisap/day_01/normalizers.py or src/aegisap/day_01/service.py?
  • Which test and rebuilt artifact prove the change is permanent?
After the code change, run the terminal verification commands below. Do not proceed until the tests are green.

Verification

Run these commands in the terminal, not in this notebook: 
uv run python -m pytest tests/test_day_01_money.py tests/test_day_01_fixtures.py -q
uv run aegisap-lab artifact rebuild --day 01
Current artifact present: build/day1/golden_thread_day1.json
